brief_justification: Paper A does deterministic semiclassical spectral analysis: it counts quasinormal-mode resonances (resolvent poles) of a Schwarzschild black hole via a new pseudodifferential calculus and complex scaling near threshold (zero) energy. Paper B fits a stochastic finite-memory multiplicative reversion process to PM2.5 time series and reads off critical-slowing-down early-warning indicators (rising autocorrelation, variance, memory, reduced resilience). The only overlap is the shared vocabulary of 'threshold/criticality'; the underlying machinery (operator resonance-counting via microlocal/elliptic theory vs. autocorrelation/variance statistics of a multiplicative stochastic process) is entirely different, so no role-for-role mapping holds.
